title: ischyrolepis pygmaea
intro: Ischyrolepis pygmaea, commonly known as the Dwarf club moss or Pygmaea club moss, is a small, rare and endangered plant species native to the Western Cape province of South Africa. It belongs to the family Lycopodiaceae and is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, including reducing stress and anxiety, improving circulation, and alleviating respiratory issues. The herbal preparation of Ischyrolepis pygmaea typically involves drying the leaves and using them in tea or infusion form, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
